Vishnu Varadhan 04/27/2020 2 .0 Updates per McLane review. Custom screen displays unloaded oLPNs in current stop . Validations are mod ified to display custom message whenever user changes the stop or temperature zone. EX 09 – RF Load Trailer Enhancements McLane leverages multi-temperature trailers for shipping which separate cooler and freezer products from ambient products. All cooler and freezer temperature products for a given shipment are loaded first across all stops for the shipment. Then all ambient temperature products for a given shipment are loaded. Within a given temperature zone, McLane requires additional sequence validations to ensure that for that temperature zone products load in stop sequence. The base WMOS load sequencing validates either stop sequence or zone sequence but not both simultaneously. Additionally, zone sequence validations in WMOS require aspects of the Yard Management solution being enabled that are not planned for McLane. To help loader identifying the oLPNs not loaded for previous stops, McLane requires a custom variance screen with oLPN , oLPN picked area and stop. In addition to the sequence validations, McLane requires that same loading option to load oLPN and as well as pallets in the following manner. When the oLPN is scanned, load only the oLPN and depalletize the oLPN if palletized. If cart pallet is scanned, load all oLPNs in cart and depalletize all the oLPNs . Finally, when the scanned pallet contains single stop and for customer requiring pallet (example: Wal-Mart ) , pallet is loaded along with all the oLPNs on the pallet . McLane also requ ires that oLPN scan for loading is captured and sent with ship confirm. WMOS enhances the RF Load Trailer transaction with custom validations against both temperature zone and stop sequence within each temperature zone. The McLane item master maintains appropriate definition for items temperature zone requirement and WMOS validates first that all products of the Cooler and Freezer temperature zones are appropriately loaded prior to allowing any ambient temperature zone products. Within a given temperature zone, WMOS validates users load the oLPNs in stop sequence. New warning messages are enabled as part of these validations to warn the user from loading out of sequence and provide appropriate information that the oLPN in question is either out of temperature or stop sequence respectively. User can override the warning by accepting the message to load the oLPN . Custom variance screen is introduced to help loaders identify missing oLPNs from previous stops within the temperature zone. User invokes the custom control key to view this custom screen. McLane requires following information to be displayed in the custom screen: stop number, oLPN number, picked warehouse area, item description for full case oLPN and stop number, oLPN number and picked warehouse area for oLPNs picked from active. When oLPN on a pallet is scanned , only that oLPN is loaded and de-palletized ; oLPN is loaded with “ oLPN scan” indicator . When a single stop pallet with order requiring pallet is scanned , WMOS retains the pallet id. In all other scenarios , WMOS nullifies the “ oLPN scan” indicator in addition to updates per b a se. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF Y RF Load Trailer – Custom oLPN sequence validation, custom variance screen and custom pallet updates Reporting N Other N At the time of oLPN scan, after pertinent base validations are performed, WMOS validates the custom Temp Zone and Stop Sequence requirement. This includes first validating that products are loaded in zone sequence. Specifically, all products labeled as Cooler or Freezer must be loaded before any product labeled as Ambient can be loaded. A custom warning message is introduced to identify exceptions when an oLPN is scanned out of temp zone sequence. Additionally, within a given temperature zone, WMOS validates that oLPNs are loaded in stop sequence, and does not allow oLPNs to load out of stop sequence. A custom warning message is introduced to identify exceptions when an oLPN is scanned out of stop sequence but within the correct temp zone. This custom validation is performed only for first oLPN of the stop is loaded with in the temperature zone. WMOS will not perform this validation when loading rest of the oLPNs for th at stop/ temperature zone and u ser WMOS will not prompt user with custom warning message . Display custom variance screen when user invokes custom control key from oLPN prompt. The variance screen should display oLPNs not loaded for the previous stops excluding the current stop that user is loading. Display stop number, oLPN number, picked warehouse area, item description for single-item oLPN from reserve or case pick and stop number, oLPN number and picked warehouse area for all multi-item oLPNs and all oLPNs picked from active. The information is sorted by stop number and then by oLPN number. The screen should be scrollable if content exceeds screen size. During loading updates, if the scanned entity is oLPN , WMOS load the oLPN and depalletize the oLPN if palletized. Update the “ oLPN scan” indicator, LPN.MISC_NUM_1 TO “1” . If pallet is scanned, load all oLPNs . D epalletize all the oLPNs per base if pallet is not a single stop retain pallet customer pallet . Otherwise, retain the pallet id by overriding transaction parameter “Floor Loading” . R etain pallet customer is identified using DO header flag sent with DO interface by the host. For all pallet scans, nullify “ oLPN scan” indicator, LPN. MISC_NUM_1 . Assumptions McLane does not leverage outbound YMS functionality and this extension does not consider any yard or trailer requirements in scope. McLane leverages RF Load Trailer for performing RF based loading. The alternative program RF Load oLPN is not in scope for this extension. Temperature control definition resides at the item master level. If for some reason, McLane defines an item as ambient but slots the item in cooler/freezer, this extension still considers the item as ambient or vice versa. If an oLPN with such item is loaded, WMOS displays custom warning message per EX09 logic . McLane host or integration system provide appropriate i I tem master flag (protection level) to identify temperature zone for each item (i.e. Cooler/Freezer vs. Ambient) is managed within WM OS by the divisions . Protection level for new items are updated through first receipt process . Items not flagged appropriately with either Cooler/Freezer or Ambient will be considered as Ambient from a load sequence perspective. McLane always picks full case single-item oLPNs only from case pick locations and reserve locations . oLPNs picked from active locations are assumed to be less than full case. They could be in a tote or carton and identifying the product based on item description may not be possible for the loader . McLane does not mix items with different protection level in same warehouse area. oLPNs are not created across multiple warehouse areas . In addition to other oLPN break attributes, McLane uses l ocation mask “ area " or “zone” to define the warehouse area by which oLPNs are cubed. McLane divisions manually re-palletize the oLPNs picked using physical cart s (not WMOS Pick Cart) into physical pallet for customers requiring pallet before loading the oLPNs to avoid retaining the cart pallet id in ship confirm. This is accomplished using RF Palletize oLPN program. RF Load Trailer parameter, “Floor loaded” is set to “Y” to de-palletize oLPN by default. Warehouse parameter “Default stop loading order” is configured as “Trailer Contents” and trailer processing sequence as “Protection Level” followed by “ Reverse Stop Sequence”. Conveyable vs non-conveyable sequence is not configured. McLane sends all or d er lines of all orders for the stop with same value of “Retain Pallet Indicator” in DO interface . All oLPNs in the pallets scanned for loading have same temperature zone items . If user scanned an oLPN in pallet by mistake, user follows the steps below to add the oLPN back to the pallet. User exits from loading program. User selects RF Unload oLPN option from the menu and scans the loaded oLPN to unload it. User exits the unloading program. User selects the RF Palletize oLPN option from the menu, scan the pallet which not loaded and the unloaded oLPN . In case of pallet scan, WMOS determines this from the oLPNs in pallet. If so, WMOS check s if scanned oLPN /pallet is out of temperature zone sequence or out of stop sequence. If out of sequence , WMOS displays custom warning message. User accepts the message. WMOS checks if the scanned entity is pallet or oLPN . If oLPN , WMOS loads the oLPN . If oLPN , WMOS performs custom update to load only the scanned oLPN . If oLPN is palletized, WMOS de-palletizes it per base configuration for transaction, “Floor Loaded” =’Y’ . WMOS updates the “ oLPN scan” indicator, LPN. MISC_NUM_1 TO “1”. If pallet, WMOS loads all oLPNs in pallet. WMOS r etains pallet only if pallet is for retain pallet customers. Otherwise, depalletizes the oLPNs per base. For all pallet scans, WMOS nullifies “ oLPN scan” indicator, LPN. MISC_NUM_1 . R etain pallet customer is identified using DO line flag sent with DO interface by the host. UI No UI changes in scope for this extension. MHE No MHE changes in scope for this extension. RF RF Load Trailer Program Platform Available Text Based (RF Gun) Y UI N Mobile (DM) N Base RF Load Trailer transaction is enhanced to perform custom temperature and stop sequence validations . A custom variance screen is designed as part of this enhancement. Additionally, WMOS retains pallets for orders/customers requiring pallet id. Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX 09 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX 09 is turned off, and no custom EX 09 processing will occur. Lastly, the custom RF transaction parameter must be enabled. This is configured in custom transaction parameter 501 - 501 . If this parameter is set to ‘ Y ’, then the custom functionality within this section is enabled. Otherwise, WMOS exits custom logic and continues per base. Screen 1 – oLPN /Pallet Prompt Display No changes to the base screen. New Custom Ctrl Keys In addition to the base control keys, following custom control key is added to invoke custom variance screen . Ctrl Key MSG # Ctrl Key Text Action Ctrl-C CTRLKEY Custom Variance Display custom variance screen *Note: McLane may configure the transaction to hide the control key Ctrl-V for base variance screen. Validations WMOS first validates the warehouse parameter “Default stop loading order”. WMOS checks if this parameter is configured as “Trailer Contents” and trailer processing sequence as “Protection Level” followed by “ Reverse Stop Sequence”. Then , WMOS checks if base transaction parameter “Floor Loaded” is set to “Y” . If both validations are passed, WMOS proceeds with next validation. Otherwise, WMOS displays custom error message CUST/0901 “WHSE/ tran parm not set properly, contact Admin”. Note: Raking value defines the protection level. Items with smallest ranking value are the items with highest protection level. PRE_PT_LVL – P rotection level of the previous oLPN /pallet loaded. Default value is highest protection level on the oLPNs in current shipment ( min ( PROTECTION_LEVEL.RANKING)) . PRE _LPN_STOP – Stop number of the previous oLPN /pallet loaded. Default value is the lowest stop on the oLPNs in current shipment . WMOS first determines last protection level and stop of last oLPN /pallet loaded for the current shipment . If previously loaded entity is oLPN (LPN. MISC_NUM_1 =”1”) , WMOS determines protection level ranking PRE_PT_LVL from oLPN details ( all items in oLPN will have same protection level ) and stop number , PRE_LPN_STOP from oLPN header (LPN.TRAILER _STOP_SEQ_NBR). If previously loaded enti ty is pallet ( LPN. REF_FIELD_8 is not blank ) , WMOS determines the oLPN with highest stop number (LPN.TRAILER_STOP_SEQ_NBR having same LPN.REF_FIELD_8 as last oLPN ) PRE_LPN_STOP and protection level ranking from PRE_PT_LVL from oLPN details for that oLPN (all items in oLPN will have same protection level and all oLPNs in the pallet have same protection level ) . If scanned entity in LPN prompt is oLPN , WMOS perform s base validations for oLPN . WMOS captures the oLPN stop LPN_STOP (LPN.TRAILER_STOP_SEQ_NBR) , and lowest protection level on oLPN details ( max ( PROTECTION_LEVEL.RANKING ) for items in oLPN ) , PT_LVL. If the user is loading for the same protection level (PT_LVL= PRE_PT_LVL ) and stop ( LPN_STOP = PRE_LPN_STOP ) , WMOS proceed s with loading updates. If the user is switching to a prior protection level (PT_LVL< PRE_PT_LVL ) or lower stop on a sa me protection level ( PT_LVL= PRE_PT_LVL and LPN_STOP < PRE_LPN_STOP ), display custom error message CUST/ 0902 “Moving from {0} stop {1} to {2} stop { 3 } , continue? ” where {0} is description for PRE_PT_LVL , {1} is PRE_LPN_STOP , {2} is description for PT_LVL and { 3 } is LPN_STOP . If user accepts the message, WMOS proceed s with loading updates. Otherwise, WMOS proceeds with next validation. I f user is switching to next protection level ( PT_LVL > PRE_PT_LVL ) or next stop ( LPN_STOP > PRE_PT_LVL and PT_LVL = PRE_PT_LVL ) , WMOS performs following custom validation s only if no other oLPN is loaded for the PT_LVL and LPN_STOP combination . If there is no oLPN on the shipment in less than loaded status with protection level ranking greater less than PT_LVL, WMOS proceed s with next validation . Otherwise, WMOS display s custom warning message CUST/ 0902 0902 “Moving from {0} stop {1} to {2} stop {3}, continue?” where {0} is description for PRE_PT_LVL , {1} is PRE_LPN_STOP , {2} is description for PT_LVL and {3} is LPN_STOP “oLPN out of temp. zone sequence” . If user accepts the message using Ctrl-A , WMOS proceeds with loading updates. If there is no oLPN on the shipment in less than loaded status with protection level ranking equal to PT_LVL and stop number greater less than LPN_STOP, WMOS proceed s with loading updates. Otherwise, WMOS display s custom warning message CUST/ 0903 0902 “Moving from {0} stop {1} to {2} stop {3}, continue?” where {0} is description for PRE_PT_LVL , {1} is PRE_LPN_STOP , {2} is description for PT_LVL and {3} is LPN_STOP “oLPN out of stop sequence” . If user accepts the message using Ctrl-A , WMOS proceeds with loading updates. If the scanned entity is pallet, WMOS perform s base validations on pallet and oLPNs in pallet. Then WMOS determines the oLPN with lowest protection level (max (PROTECTION_LEVEL.RANKING)) , PT_LVL and lowest highest stop number LPN_STOP from the oLPNs on the pallet . If the user is loading for the same protection level (PT_LVL= PRE_PT_LVL ) and stop (LPN_STOP = PRE_LPN_STOP ), WMOS proceeds with loading updates. If the user is switching to a prior protection level (PT_LVL< PRE_PT_LVL ) or lower stop on a same protection level (PT_LVL= PRE_PT_LVL and LPN_STOP < PRE_LPN_STOP ), display custom error message CUST/0902 “Moving from {0} stop {1} to {2} stop {3}, continue?” where {0} is description for PRE_PT_LVL , {1} is PRE_LPN_STOP , {2} is description for PT_LVL and {3} is LPN_STOP . If user accepts the message, WMOS proceeds with loading updates. Otherwise, WMOS proceeds with next validation. If user is switching to next protection level ( PT_LVL > PRE_PT_LVL ) or next stop ( LPN_STOP > PRE_PT_LVL and PT_LVL = PRE_PT_LVL ), WMOS performs following custom validations. WMOS performs custom validation only if no other oLPN is loaded for the PT_LVL and LPN_STOP combination. If there is no oLPN on the shipment excluding oLPNs in pallet in less than loaded status with protection level ranking greater less than PT_LVL, WMOS proceed s with next validation . Otherwise, WMOS display s custom warning message CUST/ 0902 0902 “Moving from {0} stop {1} to {2} stop {3}, continue?” where {0} is description for PRE_PT_LVL , {1} is PRE_LPN_STOP , {2} is description for PT_LVL and {3} is LPN_STOP “oLPN out of temp. zone sequence” . If user accepts the message using Ctrl-A, WMOS proceeds with loading updates. If there is no oLPN on the shipment excluding oLPNs in pallet in less than loaded status with protection level ranking equal to PT_LVL and stop number less than LPN_STOP, proceed with loading updates. Otherwise , WMOS display s custom warning message CUST/ 0903 0902 “Moving from {0} stop {1} to {2} stop {3}, continue?” where {0} is description for PRE_PT_LVL , {1} is PRE_LPN_STOP , {2} is description for PT_LVL and {3} is LPN_STOP “oLPN out of stop sequence” . If user accepts the message using Ctrl-A, WMOS proceeds with loading updates. Updates Upon successful oLPN /pallet validations , perform following updates . If scanned entity in LPN prompt is oLPN and is palletized, depalletize the oLPN and then load the oLPN per base . Additionally , update LPN. MISC_NUM_1 as “1” to indicate oLPN is scanned for loading and nullify LPN.REF_FIELD_8 . If the scanned entity is pallet, load all oLPNs in pallet. Nullify the “ oLPN scan” indicator, LPN. MISC_NUM_1 and copy the pallet id into LPN.REF_FIELD_8 . If all oLPNs in the pallet are for single shipment stop and order retain pallet flag is “ Y 1 ” ( ORDER_LINE_ITEM . REF_BOOLEAN1 ) , retain the pallet id record in WMOS by overriding transaction parameter value for “Floor Loaded” . Otherwise de-palletize the oLPNs per base . Tech Note: Fetching only one order line record of any order s for the stop should be sufficient for the retain pallet validation because all order lines for all order for the stop are expected to have the same value. EX 09 – RF Load Trailer Enhancements McLane leverages multi-temperature trailers for shipping which separate cooler and freezer products from ambient products. All cooler and freezer temperature products for a given shipment are loaded first across all stops for the shipment. Then all ambient temperature products for a given shipment are loaded. Within a given temperature zone, McLane requires additional sequence validations to ensure that for that temperature zone products load in stop sequence. The base WMOS load sequencing validates either stop sequence or zone sequence but not both simultaneously. Additionally, zone sequence validations in WMOS require aspects of the Yard Management solution being enabled that are not planned for McLane.

[Document: MCLN EX09 RF Load Trailer Enhancements 2.0]

temperature zone products load in stop sequence. The base WMOS load sequencing validates either stop sequence or zone sequence but not both simultaneously. Additionally, zone sequence validations in WMOS require aspects of the Yard Management solution being enabled that are not planned for McLane.

To help loader identifying the oLPNs not loaded for previous stops, McLane requires a custom variance screen with oLPN , oLPN picked area and stop. In addition to the sequence validations, McLane requires that same loading option to load oLPN and as well as pallets in the following manner. When the oLPN is scanned, load only the oLPN and depalletize the oLPN if palletized. If cart pallet is scanned, load all oLPNs in cart and depalletize all the oLPNs . Finally, when the scanned pallet contains single stop and for customer requiring pallet (example: Wal-Mart ) , pallet is loaded along with all the oLPNs on the pallet . McLane also requ ires that oLPN scan for loading is captured and sent with ship confirm. WMOS enhances the RF Load Trailer transaction with custom validations against both temperature zone and stop sequence within each temperature zone. The McLane item master maintains appropriate definition for items temperature zone requirement and WMOS validates first that all products of the Cooler and Freezer temperature zones are appropriately loaded prior to allowing any ambient temperature zone products. Within a given temperature zone, WMOS validates users load the oLPNs in stop sequence. New warning messages are enabled as part of these validations to warn the user from loading out of sequence and provide appropriate information that the oLPN in question is either out of temperature or stop sequence respectively. User can override the warning by accepting the message to load the oLPN . Custom variance screen is introduced to help loaders identify missing oLPNs from previous stops within the temperature zone. User invokes the custom control key to view this custom screen. McLane requires following information to be displayed in the custom screen: stop number, oLPN number, picked warehouse area, item description for full case oLPN and stop number, oLPN number and picked warehouse area for oLPNs picked from active. When oLPN on a pallet is scanned , only that oLPN is loaded and de-palletized ; oLPN is loaded with “ oLPN scan” indicator . When a single stop pallet with order requiring pallet is scanned , WMOS retains the pallet id. In all other scenarios , WMOS nullifies the “ oLPN scan” indicator in addition to updates per b a se.

Assumptions McLane does not leverage outbound YMS functionality and this extension does not consider any yard or trailer requirements in scope. McLane leverages RF Load Trailer for performing RF based loading. The alternative program RF Load oLPN is not in scope for this extension. Temperature control definition resides at the item master level. If for some reason, McLane defines an item as ambient but slots the item in cooler/freezer, this extension still considers the item as ambient or vice versa. If an oLPN with such item is loaded, WMOS displays custom warning message per EX09 logic . McLane host or integration system provide appropriate i I tem master flag (protection level) to identify temperature zone for each item (i.e. Cooler/Freezer vs. Ambient) is managed within WM OS by the divisions . Protection level for new items are updated through first receipt process . Items not flagged appropriately with either Cooler/Freezer or Ambient will be considered as Ambient from a load sequence perspective. McLane always picks full case single-item oLPNs only from case pick locations and reserve locations . oLPNs picked from active locations are assumed to be less than full case. They could be in a tote or carton and identifying the product based on item description may not be possible for the loader . McLane does not mix items with different protection level in same warehouse area. oLPNs are not created across multiple warehouse areas . In addition to other oLPN break attributes, McLane uses l ocation mask “ area " or “zone” to define the warehouse area by which oLPNs are cubed. McLane divisions manually re-palletize the oLPNs picked using physical cart s (not WMOS Pick Cart) into physical pallet for customers requiring pallet before loading the oLPNs to avoid retaining the cart pallet id in ship confirm. This is accomplished using RF Palletize oLPN program. RF Load Trailer parameter, “Floor loaded” is set to “Y” to de-palletize oLPN by default. Warehouse parameter “Default stop loading order” is configured as “Trailer Contents” and trailer processing sequence as “Protection Level” followed by “ Reverse Stop Sequence”. Conveyable vs non-conveyable sequence is not configured. McLane sends all or d er lines of all orders for the stop with same value of “Retain Pallet Indicator” in DO interface . All oLPNs in the pallets scanned for loading have same temperature zone items .

A custom variance screen is designed as part of this enhancement. Additionally, WMOS retains pallets for orders/customers requiring pallet id. Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX 09 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX 09 is turned off, and no custom EX 09 processing will occur. Lastly, the custom RF transaction parameter must be enabled. This is configured in custom transaction parameter 501 - 501 . If this parameter is set to ‘ Y ’, then the custom functionality within this section is enabled. Otherwise, WMOS exits custom logic and continues per base. Screen 1 – oLPN /Pallet Prompt Display No changes to the base screen. New Custom Ctrl Keys In addition to the base control keys, following custom control key is added to invoke custom variance screen . Ctrl Key MSG # Ctrl Key Text Action Ctrl-C CTRLKEY Custom Variance Display custom variance screen *Note: McLane may configure the transaction to hide the control key Ctrl-V for base variance screen. WMOS checks if this parameter is configured as “Trailer Contents” and trailer processing sequence as “Protection Level” followed by “ Reverse Stop Sequence”. Then , WMOS checks if base transaction parameter “Floor Loaded” is set to “Y” . If both validations are passed, WMOS proceeds with next validation. Otherwise, WMOS displays custom error message CUST/0901 “WHSE/ tran parm not set properly, contact Admin”. Note: Raking value defines the protection level. Items with smallest ranking value are the items with highest protection level. PRE_PT_LVL – P rotection level of the previous oLPN /pallet loaded.
